description: 'nomi' names the present as the span being lived, in contrast with 'luera' (past) and 'wireo' (future). Applied to a person, it can also name attentive presence in the current situation. 'nosa' (now) more narrowly locates when an act or condition occurs.
articulatory_notes: Place the tongue tip against the backs of the upper teeth for voiced dental 'n' and carry nasal airflow into stressed middle-height rounded 'o'. Close the lips for voiced 'm', keep the airflow through the nose, and finish with high-front unrounded 'i'.
usage_notes: As a predicate, 'nomi nai' says that a person is present. The word does not replace 'thesua' (mindful), which describes deliberate attention as a quality or practice.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: The present is the temporal setting in which attention, speech, and practice actually occur; naming it does not claim that attention is automatic.

semantic_domains.cognition: attentive presence in the current situation
semantic_domains.temporal: the span currently being lived between past and future
